values: Thirty Days of Fieldwork with Blue
2026-07-20 to 2026-08-18 · America/New_York · Built for a solo-founder publishing and learning loop
Mental Wealth Academy is a social-first gameworld for learning, research, and rewards.
U.S. adults ages 18–25 who already use online media for learning, psychology, self-reflection, or creator-led education
Blue Radio is the Academy's ambient platform field guide: a synchronized, character-led orientation broadcast with ten prerecorded chapters in the current episode.
Move qualified viewers into one first meaningful action: open a guide, start a mission, write a field note, inspect a quest, or tune in to Blue Radio.

Every post must teach one useful idea, demonstrate one real product action, document one learning, or ask one precise research question. Produce one master asset per day; cross-post only where the same asset fits without a second shoot or new research pass.
First meaningful action completed within 24 hours of a content click
Watch or speak with target learners
Save, question, or try the idea
Keep only topics that create useful response
The post teaches, demonstrates, documents, or asks one precise research question.
Fix any break in the route
A capability, number, testimonial, participant story, reward, price, deadline, or privacy claim lacks current proof.
Post native value plus one request
Return only with a meaningful update
Describe the current episode as prerecorded, synchronized, character-led, and roughly five minutes.
Keep education separate from diagnosis, treatment, crisis response, and promises of mental-health outcomes.
State the work and review standard before credits or a funded cash bounty. Keep infrastructure backstage.
Dates are fixed. Posting windows are test slots, not industry benchmarks. Status, owner, result, and notes are editable operating fields.
Positioning boundary: Blue Radio is an ambient platform field guide. Hold claims about live tutoring, personalization, real-time interaction, unique 24-hour programming, or learning outcomes.
Name the month and collect the audience's real questions.
What is one question you wish school had helped you investigate?

For the next 30 days, Mental Wealth Academy is running a field lab in public. One question. One useful idea. One action inside the Academy. Bring a question about psychology, media, attention, habits, or the systems shaping your choices.
Open on a blank field-note page, cut to Blue Radio, the guide map, and one mission card. End on the campaign title and date range.
Reply with one question. We will select questions that can be answered with a credible source and one useful Academy action this month.
Before posting, set a written weekly selection limit. Do not promise that every question will be answered.
Pin as the YouTube channel trailer for the month; post the question alone in Discord and one relevant community.
Define the brand idea through a concrete reflective practice.

Choose one action from today: a page of notes, a question you researched, or a decision you recorded. Write what happened and what changed. A field note turns reflection into an observation you can revisit.
One concrete action per card. Final card shows the field-note screen with observation, interpretation, and meaning.
Write one field note about an action you took today.
Present this as a reflection practice, not a clinical outcome or financial measure.
Use the first card as a Story prompt and the full carousel as a YouTube Community post.
Introduce Blue Radio with an accurate, memorable product demonstration.
Five minutes. Ten chapters. One map of the Academy.

Blue Radio is a synchronized field guide inside the Academy. The current prerecorded episode runs 4 minutes and 46 seconds across ten chapters about the course, field notes, quests, credits, the guide library, Blue, and community. Tune in anywhere in the loop, then open one feature she names.
Show the Live tab, chapter titles changing, the audio-reactive avatar, and the 4:46 runtime in a small factual label.
Tune in from the Live tab and choose one feature to open.
Label the current episode prerecorded. Avoid live tutor, interactive stream, personalized educator, and 24 hours of unique programming.
Publish a 2–5 minute YouTube walkthrough showing every chapter title.
Blue Radio tune-ins and post-listen feature opens
Show how curiosity becomes a path through the guide map.

Start with one published guide. Follow its published prerequisites back to the foundations. When you complete every direct published prerequisite for another guide, the map marks that guide ready. Your curiosity still chooses the route.
Record one real route through the public guide map. Show prerequisite labels and the next unlocked guide without exposing member data.
Open one published guide from the map.
Say 'each published topic has one canonical guide.' Do not claim every topic is verified or reviewed by PhD students.
Cut the path reveal into a 25-second vertical clip and a three-card map carousel.
Teach one media-literacy principle with a primary source.
A countdown means something only when a real condition ends.

False low-stock messages, baseless demand claims, and resetting countdowns are pressure patterns. A legitimate deadline names the event, capacity, or review window that actually closes. Inspect the next urgent screen you see: what changes when the clock reaches zero?
Show three generic interface patterns drawn from the FTC taxonomy. Use diagrams rather than copying a brand's live ad.
Inspect one urgent message today and write down what changes when its clock reaches zero.
FTC, Bringing Dark Patterns to Light (2022), pp. 20–22; research/content-calendar/source-files/ftc-bringing-dark-patterns-to-light-2022.pdf
Describe U.S. FTC staff guidance accurately. Avoid giving legal advice or accusing a named company without case-specific evidence.
Post the inspection question natively in one media-literacy community after reviewing its rules.
Yellow cells are inputs. Action rate and return rate are formulas. Define every metric before comparing posts.
